# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_is_actionable__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    actionable_pattern = re.compile('this\\s+occurs\\s+when\\s+the\\s+data\\s+are\\s+nearly\\s+identical', re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE)
    assert actionable_pattern.search(source), "Expected the moment-precision-loss RuntimeWarning message to include an actionable condition for users ('This occurs when the data are nearly identical.')."

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_is_actionable__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    actionable_pattern = re.compile('this\\s+occurs\\s+when\\s+the\\s+data\\s+are\\s+nearly\\s+identical', re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE)
    assert re.search('catastrophic\\s+cancellation', source, re.IGNORECASE), "Expected the moment-precision-loss warning message to contain the corrected phrase 'catastrophic cancellation'."

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_is_actionable__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    actionable_pattern = re.compile('this\\s+occurs\\s+when\\s+the\\s+data\\s+are\\s+nearly\\s+identical', re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE)
    assert 'catastrophc cancellation' not in source, "Found the old misspelled warning text 'catastrophc cancellation' in the source; it should have been replaced."

            np.core.multiarray.normalize_axis_index(axis, a.ndim)
            dt = np.float64 if method == 'average' else np.int_
            return np.empty(a.shape, dtype=dt)
        return np.apply_along_axis(rankdata, axis, a, method)

    arr = np.ravel(np.asarray(a))
    algo = 'mergesort' if method == 'ordinal' else 'quicksort'
    sorter = np.argsort(arr, kind=algo)

    inv = np.empty(sorter.size, dtype=np.intp)
    inv[sorter] = np.arange(sorter.size, dtype=np.intp)

    if method == 'ordinal':
        return inv + 1

    arr = arr[sorter]
    obs = np.r_[True, arr[1:] != arr[:-1]]
    dense = obs.cumsum()[inv]

    if method == 'dense':
        return dense

    # cumulative counts of each unique value
    count = np.r_[np.nonzero(obs)[0], len(obs)]

    if method == 'max':
        return count[dense]

    if method == 'min':
        return count[dense - 1] + 1

    # average method
    return .5 * (count[dense] + count[dense - 1] + 1)

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_spelling_and_context__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'catastrophic cancellation' in source, "Expected the moment() precision-loss warning to contain the phrase 'catastrophic cancellation' (correct spelling)."

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_spelling_and_context__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'nearly identical' in source, 'Expected the moment() precision-loss warning to include context that this occurs when the data are nearly identical.'

def test_moment_precision_loss_warning_message_spelling_and_context__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'catastrophc cancellation' not in source, "Found old misspelled phrase 'catastrophc cancellation' in the source; it should be corrected to 'catastrophic cancellation'."

def test_moment_precision_loss_detection_logic_uses_abs_mean_and_eps_10__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('np\\.finfo\\(\\s*a_zero_mean\\.dtype\\s*\\)\\.resolution\\s*\\*\\s*10\\b', source), "Expected eps computation to scale resolution by 10 (i.e., '* 10'), as in the updated precision-loss detection."

def test_moment_precision_loss_detection_logic_uses_abs_mean_and_eps_10__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('/\\s*np\\.abs\\(\\s*mean\\s*\\)', source), "Expected relative difference to be computed by dividing by np.abs(mean) (i.e., '/ np.abs(mean)') to avoid sign/zero issues."

def test_moment_precision_loss_detection_logic_uses_abs_mean_and_eps_10__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'resolution * 1e6' not in source, "Found old eps scaling 'resolution * 1e6' in the source; expected updated scaling 'resolution * 10'."

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_moment_precision_loss_heuristic_and_message_updated__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'np.finfo(a_zero_mean.dtype).resolution * 10' in source, 'Expected _moment precision-loss check to use a much smaller tolerance (resolution * 10). This ensures warnings only occur on nearly-identical data, not on valid inputs.'

def test_moment_precision_loss_heuristic_and_message_updated__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert '/ np.abs(mean)' in source or '/np.abs(mean)' in source, 'Expected _moment relative difference to divide by np.abs(mean) to avoid incorrect behavior when mean is negative.'

def test_moment_precision_loss_heuristic_and_message_updated__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('precision_loss\\s*=\\s*np\\.any\\(\\s*rel_diff\\s*<\\s*eps\\s*\\)', source), 'Expected _moment precision-loss condition to be `np.any(rel_diff < eps)` (no extra `0 < rel_diff` guard).'

def test_moment_precision_loss_heuristic_and_message_updated__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'catastrophic cancellation' in source, "Expected precision-loss warning message to contain the correctly spelled 'catastrophic cancellation'."

def test_moment_precision_loss_heuristic_and_message_updated__a5():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'nearly identical' in source, 'Expected precision-loss warning message to explain it occurs when the data are nearly identical.'

results.comment_text:
@author:
```suggestion
            precision_loss = np.any(rel_diff < eps)
```
We probably _do_ want this warning to show when all the data is exactly numerically equal. Related: I don't think it was correct in gh-13254 to set the `skew`/`kurtosis` to zero when all data were equal; it should be `nan`, and there should be a warning.

@user1:
> We probably do want this warning to show when all the data is exactly numerically equal.

Yes, agreed.

> I don't think it was correct in [gh-13254](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/13254) to set the `skew`/`kurtosis` to zero when all data were equal; it should be `nan`, and there should be a warning.

I agree: 0 is not the correct skew/kurtosis for near-constant inputs.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'precision_loss = np.any(rel_diff < eps)' in source, 'Regression test for constant-input precision-loss detection: expected `precision_loss = np.any(rel_diff < eps)` so that the warning triggers even when rel_diff == 0 (exactly constant/identical data).'

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'precision_loss = np.any((0 < rel_diff) & (rel_diff < eps))' not in source, 'The old logic required `0 < rel_diff`, which suppresses the precision-loss warning when inputs are exactly constant; this should be removed.'

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'catastrophic cancellation' in source, "Expected warning message to mention 'catastrophic cancellation' (spelling fixed)."

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'catastrophc cancellation' not in source, "Old warning message contained typo 'catastrophc cancellation'; should be fixed."

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a5():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'nearly identical' in source, 'Expected warning message to explain that catastrophic cancellation occurs when the data are nearly identical.'

def test__moment_precision_loss_detects_exact_constant_inputs_and_warning_message_spelling__a6():
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/stats/_stats_py.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('def _moment\\([\\s\\S]*?precision_loss', source), 'Test expects `_moment` to contain precision-loss detection logic.'
